Uge Shrine |画像 = [[File:Uge-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499}}{{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835}}Kannari 1178 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|46.35|N|138|49|59.71|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uge Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} era ({{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Uge Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uge Shrine''' (うげじんじゃ; {{ill|lt=kyūjitai|Q1147857|WD=Q1147857}}: 宇藝神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kannari, {{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami}} (うかのみたまのかみ){{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Tomioka City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Tomioka City |page=421 |doi=10.11501/9643274 |editor=Tomioka City History Editorial Committee |volume=Folklore volume}}{{要登録}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the Hakuhō era (late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The present shrine site was originally where Akagi Shrine stood, and Uge Shrine was originally on the west side near Yoshida Pond{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as "Uge Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kanra District|Q1076986|WD=Q1076986}}, {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=40}}. In the ''Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō'', both the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Nukisaki Shrine|Q10863796|WD=Q10863796|lt=Ichinomiya}} manuscript and the {{ill|lt=Gunsho Ruijū|Q1554852|WD=Q1554852}} manuscript include "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin" among the 12 guardian shrines of Kōzuke, and this is identified with this shrine. On the other hand, the {{ill|lt=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)|Q17220813|WD=Q17220813|lt=Sōja}} manuscript does not include it among the 10 guardian shrines. In the Kanra District section, the Ichinomiya manuscript has "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin," while the Gunsho Ruijū and Sōja manuscripts have "Junior First Rank, Sōgi Myōjin." {{ill|lt=Ozaki Kisao|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487}} regarded the guardian-shrine entry and district entry as different shrines, assigning the latter to Soki Shrine (Soki, Tomioka){{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=46,192-196}}. It was destroyed by fire during the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121}} era ({{ill|lt=1781|Q7719|WD=Q7719}}-{{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772}}), and the current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in April of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892}}){{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1801|Q6877|WD=Q6877}})?{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The festival date was November 3, but is now April 3 and October 15. === Precincts === * Honden - one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}. * Haiden * Mukuroji tree - 14 m tall, about 300 years old. Designated natural monument of Tomioka City. * Precinct shrines ** Akagi Shrine ** Takaokami Shrine ** Santai Shrine ** Shinmeigū ** Mishima Shrine ** Fuji Shrine ** Kotohira-gū ** Hokuto Shrine ** Ōikazuchi Shrine File:Uge-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Uge-jinja, shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Uge-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii File:Uge-jinja, mukuroji.jpg|Mukuroji tree (Tomioka City designated natural monument) ファイル:宇芸神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Cultural properties === === Tomioka City designated cultural properties === * Natural monument ** Mukuroji tree of Uge Shrine - designated April 28, 2010 (Heisei 22)[https://www.city.tomioka.lg.jp/www/contents/1000000000185/index.html Mukuroji Tree of Uge Shrine] (Tomioka City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Study of the Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |last=Ozaki |author=Ozaki Kisao |first=Kisao |author-link=尾崎喜左雄 |doi=10.11501/12266236 |ref=harv}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kita-Kanra District, Gunma Prefecture |date=1928-11-1 |year=1928 |publisher=Tamura Yasutarō |ref=harv |last=Honda |author=Honda Kamezō |first=Kamezō |doi=10.11501/3440451}}{{要登録}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Uge-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|Q11360308|WD=Q11360308}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うけしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uge Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:富岡市の建築物]] [[Category:富岡市の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Uge Shrine |画像 = [[File:Uge-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499}}{{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835}}Kannari 1178 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|46.35|N|138|49|59.71|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uge Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}} era ({{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Uge Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uge Shrine''' (うげじんじゃ; {{ill|lt=kyūjitai|Q1147857|WD=Q1147857}}: 宇藝神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kannari, {{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami}} (うかのみたまのかみ){{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Tomioka City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Tomioka City |page=421 |doi=10.11501/9643274 |editor=Tomioka City History Editorial Committee |volume=Folklore volume}}{{要登録}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the Hakuhō era (late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The present shrine site was originally where Akagi Shrine stood, and Uge Shrine was originally on the west side near Yoshida Pond{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as "Uge Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kanra District|Q1076986|WD=Q1076986}}, {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=40}}. In the ''Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō'', both the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Nukisaki Shrine|Q10863796|WD=Q10863796|lt=Ichinomiya}} manuscript and the {{ill|lt=Gunsho Ruijū|Q1554852|WD=Q1554852}} manuscript include "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin" among the 12 guardian shrines of Kōzuke, and this is identified with this shrine. On the other hand, the {{ill|lt=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)|Q17220813|WD=Q17220813|lt=Sōja}} manuscript does not include it among the 10 guardian shrines. In the Kanra District section, the Ichinomiya manuscript has "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin," while the Gunsho Ruijū and Sōja manuscripts have "Junior First Rank, Sōgi Myōjin." {{ill|lt=Ozaki Kisao|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487}} regarded the guardian-shrine entry and district entry as different shrines, assigning the latter to Soki Shrine (Soki, Tomioka){{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=46,192-196}}. It was destroyed by fire during the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121}} era ({{ill|lt=1781|Q7719|WD=Q7719}}-{{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772}}), and the current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in April of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892}}){{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1801|Q6877|WD=Q6877}})?{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The festival date was November 3, but is now April 3 and October 15. === Precincts === * Honden - one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}. * Haiden * Mukuroji tree - 14 m tall, about 300 years old. Designated natural monument of Tomioka City. * Precinct shrines ** Akagi Shrine ** Takaokami Shrine ** Santai Shrine ** Shinmeigū ** Mishima Shrine ** Fuji Shrine ** Kotohira-gū ** Hokuto Shrine ** Ōikazuchi Shrine File:Uge-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Uge-jinja, shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Uge-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii File:Uge-jinja, mukuroji.jpg|Mukuroji tree (Tomioka City designated natural monument) ファイル:宇芸神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Cultural properties === === Tomioka City designated cultural properties === * Natural monument ** Mukuroji tree of Uge Shrine - designated April 28, 2010 (Heisei 22)[https://www.city.tomioka.lg.jp/www/contents/1000000000185/index.html Mukuroji Tree of Uge Shrine] (Tomioka City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Study of the Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |last=Ozaki |author=Ozaki Kisao |first=Kisao |author-link=尾崎喜左雄 |doi=10.11501/12266236 |ref=harv}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kita-Kanra District, Gunma Prefecture |date=1928-11-1 |year=1928 |publisher=Tamura Yasutarō |ref=harv |last=Honda |author=Honda Kamezō |first=Kamezō |doi=10.11501/3440451}}{{要登録}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Uge-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|Q11360308|WD=Q11360308}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うけしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uge Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:富岡市の建築物]] [[Category:富岡市の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ){{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Tomioka City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Tomioka City |page=421 |doi=10.11501/9643274 |editor=Tomioka City History Editorial Committee |volume=Folklore volume}}{{要登録}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the Hakuhō era (late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The present shrine site was originally where Akagi Shrine stood, and Uge Shrine was originally on the west side near Yoshida Pond{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Uge Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kanra District|Q1076986|WD=Q1076986|1=Kanra District, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=40}}. In the ''Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō'', both the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Nukisaki Shrine|Q10863796|WD=Q10863796|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Nukisaki Shrine}} manuscript and the {{ill|lt=Gunsho Ruijū|Q1554852|WD=Q1554852|1=Gunsho Ruijū}} manuscript include "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin" among the 12 guardian shrines of Kōzuke, and this is identified with this shrine. On the other hand, the {{ill|lt=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)|Q17220813|WD=Q17220813|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)}} manuscript does not include it among the 10 guardian shrines. In the Kanra District section, the Ichinomiya manuscript has "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin," while the Gunsho Ruijū and Sōja manuscripts have "Junior First Rank, Sōgi Myōjin." {{ill|lt=Ozaki Kisao|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} regarded the guardian-shrine entry and district entry as different shrines, assigning the latter to Soki Shrine (Soki, Tomioka){{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=46,192-196}}. It was destroyed by fire during the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} era ({{ill|lt=1781|Q7719|WD=Q7719|1=1781}}-{{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772|1=1789}}), and the current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in April of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}){{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1801|Q6877|WD=Q6877|1=1801}})?{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The festival date was November 3, but is now April 3 and October 15. === Precincts === * Honden - one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. * Haiden * Mukuroji tree - 14 m tall, about 300 years old. Designated natural monument of Tomioka City. * Precinct shrines ** Akagi Shrine ** Takaokami Shrine ** Santai Shrine ** Shinmeigū ** Mishima Shrine ** Fuji Shrine ** Kotohira-gū ** Hokuto Shrine ** Ōikazuchi Shrine File:Uge-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Uge-jinja, shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Uge-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii File:Uge-jinja, mukuroji.jpg|Mukuroji tree (Tomioka City designated natural monument) ファイル:宇芸神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Cultural properties === === Tomioka City designated cultural properties === * Natural monument ** Mukuroji tree of Uge Shrine - designated April 28, 2010 (Heisei 22)[https://www.city.tomioka.lg.jp/www/contents/1000000000185/index.html Mukuroji Tree of Uge Shrine] (Tomioka City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Study of the Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |last=Ozaki |author=Ozaki Kisao |first=Kisao |author-link=尾崎喜左雄 |doi=10.11501/12266236 |ref=harv}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kita-Kanra District, Gunma Prefecture |date=1928-11-1 |year=1928 |publisher=Tamura Yasutarō |ref=harv |last=Honda |author=Honda Kamezō |first=Kamezō |doi=10.11501/3440451}}{{要登録}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Uge-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|Q11360308|WD=Q11360308|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うけしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uge Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:富岡市の建築物]] [[Category:富岡市の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Uge Shrine |画像 = [[File:Uge-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835|1=Tomioka, Gunma}}Kannari 1178 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|46.35|N|138|49|59.71|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uge Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} era ({{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Uge Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uge Shrine''' (うげじんじゃ; {{ill|lt=kyūjitai|Q1147857|WD=Q1147857|1=Kyūjitai}}: 宇藝神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kannari, {{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835|1=Tomioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ){{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Tomioka City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Tomioka City |page=421 |doi=10.11501/9643274 |editor=Tomioka City History Editorial Committee |volume=Folklore volume}}{{要登録}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the Hakuhō era (late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The present shrine site was originally where Akagi Shrine stood, and Uge Shrine was originally on the west side near Yoshida Pond{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Uge Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kanra District|Q1076986|WD=Q1076986|1=Kanra District, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=40}}. In the ''Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō'', both the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Nukisaki Shrine|Q10863796|WD=Q10863796|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Nukisaki Shrine}} manuscript and the {{ill|lt=Gunsho Ruijū|Q1554852|WD=Q1554852|1=Gunsho Ruijū}} manuscript include "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin" among the 12 guardian shrines of Kōzuke, and this is identified with this shrine. On the other hand, the {{ill|lt=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)|Q17220813|WD=Q17220813|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)}} manuscript does not include it among the 10 guardian shrines. In the Kanra District section, the Ichinomiya manuscript has "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin," while the Gunsho Ruijū and Sōja manuscripts have "Junior First Rank, Sōgi Myōjin." {{ill|lt=Ozaki Kisao|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} regarded the guardian-shrine entry and district entry as different shrines, assigning the latter to Soki Shrine (Soki, Tomioka){{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=46,192-196}}. It was destroyed by fire during the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} era ({{ill|lt=1781|Q7719|WD=Q7719|1=1781}}-{{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772|1=1789}}), and the current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in April of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}){{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1801|Q6877|WD=Q6877|1=1801}})?{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The festival date was November 3, but is now April 3 and October 15. === Precincts === * Honden - one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. * Haiden * Mukuroji tree - 14 m tall, about 300 years old. Designated natural monument of Tomioka City. * Precinct shrines ** Akagi Shrine ** Takaokami Shrine ** Santai Shrine ** Shinmeigū ** Mishima Shrine ** Fuji Shrine ** Kotohira-gū ** Hokuto Shrine ** Ōikazuchi Shrine File:Uge-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Uge-jinja, shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Uge-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii File:Uge-jinja, mukuroji.jpg|Mukuroji tree (Tomioka City designated natural monument) ファイル:宇芸神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Cultural properties === === Tomioka City designated cultural properties === * Natural monument ** Mukuroji tree of Uge Shrine - designated April 28, 2010 (Heisei 22)[https://www.city.tomioka.lg.jp/www/contents/1000000000185/index.html Mukuroji Tree of Uge Shrine] (Tomioka City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Study of the Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |last=Ozaki |author=Ozaki Kisao |first=Kisao |author-link=尾崎喜左雄 |doi=10.11501/12266236 |ref=harv}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kita-Kanra District, Gunma Prefecture |date=1928-11-1 |year=1928 |publisher=Tamura Yasutarō |ref=harv |last=Honda |author=Honda Kamezō |first=Kamezō |doi=10.11501/3440451}}{{要登録}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Uge-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|Q11360308|WD=Q11360308|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うけしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uge Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:富岡市の建築物]] [[Category:富岡市の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Uge Shrine |画像 = [[File:Uge-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835|1=Tomioka, Gunma}}Kannari 1178 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|46.35|N|138|49|59.71|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uge Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} era ({{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Uge Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uge Shrine''' (うげじんじゃ; {{ill|lt=kyūjitai|Q1147857|WD=Q1147857|1=Kyūjitai}}: 宇藝神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kannari, {{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835|1=Tomioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ){{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Tomioka City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Tomioka City |page=421 |doi=10.11501/9643274 |editor=Tomioka City History Editorial Committee |volume=Folklore volume}}{{要登録}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the Hakuhō era (late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The present shrine site was originally where Akagi Shrine stood, and Uge Shrine was originally on the west side near Yoshida Pond{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Uge Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kanra District|Q1076986|WD=Q1076986|1=Kanra District, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=40}}. In the ''Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō'', both the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Nukisaki Shrine|Q10863796|WD=Q10863796|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Nukisaki Shrine}} manuscript and the {{ill|lt=Gunsho Ruijū|Q1554852|WD=Q1554852|1=Gunsho Ruijū}} manuscript include "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin" among the 12 guardian shrines of Kōzuke, and this is identified with this shrine. On the other hand, the {{ill|lt=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)|Q17220813|WD=Q17220813|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)}} manuscript does not include it among the 10 guardian shrines. In the Kanra District section, the Ichinomiya manuscript has "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin," while the Gunsho Ruijū and Sōja manuscripts have "Junior First Rank, Sōgi Myōjin." {{ill|lt=Ozaki Kisao|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} regarded the guardian-shrine entry and district entry as different shrines, assigning the latter to Soki Shrine (Soki, Tomioka){{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=46,192-196}}. It was destroyed by fire during the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} era ({{ill|lt=1781|Q7719|WD=Q7719|1=1781}}-{{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772|1=1789}}), and the current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in April of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}){{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1801|Q6877|WD=Q6877|1=1801}})?{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The festival date was November 3, but is now April 3 and October 15. === Precincts === * Honden - one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. * Haiden * Mukuroji tree - 14 m tall, about 300 years old. Designated natural monument of Tomioka City. * Precinct shrines ** Akagi Shrine ** Takaokami Shrine ** Santai Shrine ** Shinmeigū ** Mishima Shrine ** Fuji Shrine ** Kotohira-gū ** Hokuto Shrine ** Ōikazuchi Shrine File:Uge-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Uge-jinja, shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Uge-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii File:Uge-jinja, mukuroji.jpg|Mukuroji tree (Tomioka City designated natural monument) ファイル:宇芸神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Cultural properties === === Tomioka City designated cultural properties === * Natural monument ** Mukuroji tree of Uge Shrine - designated April 28, 2010 (Heisei 22)[https://www.city.tomioka.lg.jp/www/contents/1000000000185/index.html Mukuroji Tree of Uge Shrine] (Tomioka City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Study of the Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |last=Ozaki |author=Ozaki Kisao |first=Kisao |author-link=尾崎喜左雄 |doi=10.11501/12266236 |ref=harv}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kita-Kanra District, Gunma Prefecture |date=1928-11-1 |year=1928 |publisher=Tamura Yasutarō |ref=harv |last=Honda |author=Honda Kamezō |first=Kamezō |doi=10.11501/3440451}}{{要登録}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Uge-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|Q11360308|WD=Q11360308|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うけしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uge Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:富岡市の建築物]] [[Category:富岡市の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ){{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Tomioka City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Tomioka City |page=421 |doi=10.11501/9643274 |editor=Tomioka City History Editorial Committee |volume=Folklore volume}}{{要登録}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the Hakuhō era (late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The present shrine site was originally where Akagi Shrine stood, and Uge Shrine was originally on the west side near Yoshida Pond{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Uge Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kanra District|Q1076986|WD=Q1076986|1=Kanra District, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=40}}. In the ''Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō'', both the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Nukisaki Shrine|Q10863796|WD=Q10863796|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Nukisaki Shrine}} manuscript and the {{ill|lt=Gunsho Ruijū|Q1554852|WD=Q1554852|1=Gunsho Ruijū}} manuscript include "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin" among the 12 guardian shrines of Kōzuke, and this is identified with this shrine. On the other hand, the {{ill|lt=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)|Q17220813|WD=Q17220813|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)}} manuscript does not include it among the 10 guardian shrines. In the Kanra District section, the Ichinomiya manuscript has "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin," while the Gunsho Ruijū and Sōja manuscripts have "Junior First Rank, Sōgi Myōjin." {{ill|lt=Ozaki Kisao|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} regarded the guardian-shrine entry and district entry as different shrines, assigning the latter to Soki Shrine (Soki, Tomioka){{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=46,192-196}}. It was destroyed by fire during the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} era ({{ill|lt=1781|Q7719|WD=Q7719|1=1781}}-{{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772|1=1789}}), and the current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in April of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}){{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1801|Q6877|WD=Q6877|1=1801}})?{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The festival date was November 3, but is now April 3 and October 15. === Precincts === * Honden - one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. * Haiden * Mukuroji tree - 14 m tall, about 300 years old. Designated natural monument of Tomioka City. * Precinct shrines ** Akagi Shrine ** Takaokami Shrine ** Santai Shrine ** Shinmeigū ** Mishima Shrine ** Fuji Shrine ** Kotohira-gū ** Hokuto Shrine ** Ōikazuchi Shrine File:Uge-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Uge-jinja, shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Uge-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii File:Uge-jinja, mukuroji.jpg|Mukuroji tree (Tomioka City designated natural monument) ファイル:宇芸神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Cultural properties === === Tomioka City designated cultural properties === * Natural monument ** Mukuroji tree of Uge Shrine - designated April 28, 2010 (Heisei 22)[https://www.city.tomioka.lg.jp/www/contents/1000000000185/index.html Mukuroji Tree of Uge Shrine] (Tomioka City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Study of the Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |last=Ozaki |author=Ozaki Kisao |first=Kisao |author-link=尾崎喜左雄 |doi=10.11501/12266236 |ref=harv}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kita-Kanra District, Gunma Prefecture |date=1928-11-1 |year=1928 |publisher=Tamura Yasutarō |ref=harv |last=Honda |author=Honda Kamezō |first=Kamezō |doi=10.11501/3440451}}{{要登録}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Uge-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|Q11360308|WD=Q11360308|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うけしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uge Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:富岡市の建築物]] [[Category:富岡市の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Uge-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '群馬県富岡市神成1178', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Uge-jinja}} [[JA:宇芸神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11449611}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uge Shrine |画像 = [[File:Uge-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835|1=Tomioka, Gunma}}Kannari 1178 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|46.35|N|138|49|59.71|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uge Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} era ({{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Uge Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uge Shrine''' (うげじんじゃ; {{ill|lt=kyūjitai|Q1147857|WD=Q1147857|1=Kyūjitai}}: 宇藝神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kannari, {{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835|1=Tomioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ){{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Tomioka City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Tomioka City |page=421 |doi=10.11501/9643274 |editor=Tomioka City History Editorial Committee |volume=Folklore volume}}{{要登録}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the Hakuhō era (late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The present shrine site was originally where Akagi Shrine stood, and Uge Shrine was originally on the west side near Yoshida Pond{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Uge Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kanra District|Q1076986|WD=Q1076986|1=Kanra District, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=40}}. In the ''Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō'', both the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Nukisaki Shrine|Q10863796|WD=Q10863796|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Nukisaki Shrine}} manuscript and the {{ill|lt=Gunsho Ruijū|Q1554852|WD=Q1554852|1=Gunsho Ruijū}} manuscript include "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin" among the 12 guardian shrines of Kōzuke, and this is identified with this shrine. On the other hand, the {{ill|lt=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)|Q17220813|WD=Q17220813|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)}} manuscript does not include it among the 10 guardian shrines. In the Kanra District section, the Ichinomiya manuscript has "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin," while the Gunsho Ruijū and Sōja manuscripts have "Junior First Rank, Sōgi Myōjin." {{ill|lt=Ozaki Kisao|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} regarded the guardian-shrine entry and district entry as different shrines, assigning the latter to Soki Shrine (Soki, Tomioka){{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=46,192-196}}. It was destroyed by fire during the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} era ({{ill|lt=1781|Q7719|WD=Q7719|1=1781}}-{{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772|1=1789}}), and the current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in April of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}){{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1801|Q6877|WD=Q6877|1=1801}})?{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The festival date was November 3, but is now April 3 and October 15. === Precincts === * Honden - one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. * Haiden * Mukuroji tree - 14 m tall, about 300 years old. Designated natural monument of Tomioka City. * Precinct shrines ** Akagi Shrine ** Takaokami Shrine ** Santai Shrine ** Shinmeigū ** Mishima Shrine ** Fuji Shrine ** Kotohira-gū ** Hokuto Shrine ** Ōikazuchi Shrine File:Uge-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Uge-jinja, shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Uge-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii File:Uge-jinja, mukuroji.jpg|Mukuroji tree (Tomioka City designated natural monument) ファイル:宇芸神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Cultural properties === === Tomioka City designated cultural properties === * Natural monument ** Mukuroji tree of Uge Shrine - designated April 28, 2010 (Heisei 22)[https://www.city.tomioka.lg.jp/www/contents/1000000000185/index.html Mukuroji Tree of Uge Shrine] (Tomioka City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Study of the Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |last=Ozaki |author=Ozaki Kisao |first=Kisao |author-link=尾崎喜左雄 |doi=10.11501/12266236 |ref=harv}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kita-Kanra District, Gunma Prefecture |date=1928-11-1 |year=1928 |publisher=Tamura Yasutarō |ref=harv |last=Honda |author=Honda Kamezō |first=Kamezō |doi=10.11501/3440451}}{{要登録}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Uge-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|Q11360308|WD=Q11360308|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uge Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:富岡市の建築物]] [[Category:富岡市の歴史]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835|1=Tomioka, Gunma}}Kannari 1178 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|46.35|N|138|49|59.71|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uge Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} era ({{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Uge Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uge Shrine''' (うげじんじゃ; {{ill|lt=kyūjitai|Q1147857|WD=Q1147857|1=Kyūjitai}}: 宇藝神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kannari, {{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835|1=Tomioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ){{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Tomioka City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Tomioka City |page=421 |doi=10.11501/9643274 |editor=Tomioka City History Editorial Committee |volume=Folklore volume}}{{要登録}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the Hakuhō era (late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The present shrine site was originally where Akagi Shrine stood, and Uge Shrine was originally on the west side near Yoshida Pond{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Uge Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kanra District|Q1076986|WD=Q1076986|1=Kanra District, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=40}}. In the ''Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō'', both the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Nukisaki Shrine|Q10863796|WD=Q10863796|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Nukisaki Shrine}} manuscript and the {{ill|lt=Gunsho Ruijū|Q1554852|WD=Q1554852|1=Gunsho Ruijū}} manuscript include "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin" among the 12 guardian shrines of Kōzuke, and this is identified with this shrine. On the other hand, the {{ill|lt=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)|Q17220813|WD=Q17220813|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)}} manuscript does not include it among the 10 guardian shrines. In the Kanra District section, the Ichinomiya manuscript has "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin," while the Gunsho Ruijū and Sōja manuscripts have "Junior First Rank, Sōgi Myōjin." {{ill|lt=Ozaki Kisao|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} regarded the guardian-shrine entry and district entry as different shrines, assigning the latter to Soki Shrine (Soki, Tomioka){{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=46,192-196}}. It was destroyed by fire during the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} era ({{ill|lt=1781|Q7719|WD=Q7719|1=1781}}-{{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772|1=1789}}), and the current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in April of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}){{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1801|Q6877|WD=Q6877|1=1801}})?{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The festival date was November 3, but is now April 3 and October 15. === Precincts === * Honden - one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. * Haiden * Mukuroji tree - 14 m tall, about 300 years old. Designated natural monument of Tomioka City. * Precinct shrines ** Akagi Shrine ** Takaokami Shrine ** Santai Shrine ** Shinmeigū ** Mishima Shrine ** Fuji Shrine ** Kotohira-gū ** Hokuto Shrine ** Ōikazuchi Shrine File:Uge-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Uge-jinja, shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Uge-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii File:Uge-jinja, mukuroji.jpg|Mukuroji tree (Tomioka City designated natural monument) ファイル:宇芸神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Cultural properties === === Tomioka City designated cultural properties === * Natural monument ** Mukuroji tree of Uge Shrine - designated April 28, 2010 (Heisei 22)[https://www.city.tomioka.lg.jp/www/contents/1000000000185/index.html Mukuroji Tree of Uge Shrine] (Tomioka City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Study of the Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |last=Ozaki |author=Ozaki Kisao |first=Kisao |author-link=尾崎喜左雄 |doi=10.11501/12266236 |ref=harv}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kita-Kanra District, Gunma Prefecture |date=1928-11-1 |year=1928 |publisher=Tamura Yasutarō |ref=harv |last=Honda |author=Honda Kamezō |first=Kamezō |doi=10.11501/3440451}}{{要登録}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Uge-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|Q11360308|WD=Q11360308|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uge Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:富岡市の建築物]] [[Category:富岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} era ({{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Uge Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uge Shrine''' (うげじんじゃ; {{ill|lt=kyūjitai|Q1147857|WD=Q1147857|1=Kyūjitai}}: 宇藝神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kannari, {{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835|1=Tomioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ){{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Tomioka City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Tomioka City |page=421 |doi=10.11501/9643274 |editor=Tomioka City History Editorial Committee |volume=Folklore volume}}{{要登録}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the Hakuhō era (late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The present shrine site was originally where Akagi Shrine stood, and Uge Shrine was originally on the west side near Yoshida Pond{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Uge Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kanra District|Q1076986|WD=Q1076986|1=Kanra District, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=40}}. In the ''Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō'', both the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Nukisaki Shrine|Q10863796|WD=Q10863796|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Nukisaki Shrine}} manuscript and the {{ill|lt=Gunsho Ruijū|Q1554852|WD=Q1554852|1=Gunsho Ruijū}} manuscript include "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin" among the 12 guardian shrines of Kōzuke, and this is identified with this shrine. On the other hand, the {{ill|lt=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)|Q17220813|WD=Q17220813|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)}} manuscript does not include it among the 10 guardian shrines. In the Kanra District section, the Ichinomiya manuscript has "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin," while the Gunsho Ruijū and Sōja manuscripts have "Junior First Rank, Sōgi Myōjin." {{ill|lt=Ozaki Kisao|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} regarded the guardian-shrine entry and district entry as different shrines, assigning the latter to Soki Shrine (Soki, Tomioka){{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=46,192-196}}. It was destroyed by fire during the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} era ({{ill|lt=1781|Q7719|WD=Q7719|1=1781}}-{{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772|1=1789}}), and the current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in April of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}){{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1801|Q6877|WD=Q6877|1=1801}})?{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The festival date was November 3, but is now April 3 and October 15. === Precincts === * Honden - one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. * Haiden * Mukuroji tree - 14 m tall, about 300 years old. Designated natural monument of Tomioka City. * Precinct shrines ** Akagi Shrine ** Takaokami Shrine ** Santai Shrine ** Shinmeigū ** Mishima Shrine ** Fuji Shrine ** Kotohira-gū ** Hokuto Shrine ** Ōikazuchi Shrine File:Uge-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Uge-jinja, shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Uge-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii File:Uge-jinja, mukuroji.jpg|Mukuroji tree (Tomioka City designated natural monument) ファイル:宇芸神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Cultural properties === === Tomioka City designated cultural properties === * Natural monument ** Mukuroji tree of Uge Shrine - designated April 28, 2010 (Heisei 22)[https://www.city.tomioka.lg.jp/www/contents/1000000000185/index.html Mukuroji Tree of Uge Shrine] (Tomioka City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Study of the Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |last=Ozaki |author=Ozaki Kisao |first=Kisao |author-link=尾崎喜左雄 |doi=10.11501/12266236 |ref=harv}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kita-Kanra District, Gunma Prefecture |date=1928-11-1 |year=1928 |publisher=Tamura Yasutarō |ref=harv |last=Honda |author=Honda Kamezō |first=Kamezō |doi=10.11501/3440451}}{{要登録}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Uge-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|Q11360308|WD=Q11360308|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uge Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:富岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tomioka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Uge-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '群馬県富岡市神成1178', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Uge-jinja}} [[JA:宇芸神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11449611}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uge Shrine |画像 = [[File:Uge-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835|1=Tomioka, Gunma}}Kannari 1178 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|14|46.35|N|138|49|59.71|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uge Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} era ({{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Uge Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uge Shrine''' (うげじんじゃ; {{ill|lt=kyūjitai|Q1147857|WD=Q1147857|1=Kyūjitai}}: 宇藝神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kannari, {{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835|1=Tomioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ){{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Tomioka City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Tomioka City |page=421 |doi=10.11501/9643274 |editor=Tomioka City History Editorial Committee |volume=Folklore volume}}{{要登録}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the Hakuhō era (late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The present shrine site was originally where Akagi Shrine stood, and Uge Shrine was originally on the west side near Yoshida Pond{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Uge Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kanra District|Q1076986|WD=Q1076986|1=Kanra District, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=40}}. In the ''Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō'', both the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Nukisaki Shrine|Q10863796|WD=Q10863796|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Nukisaki Shrine}} manuscript and the {{ill|lt=Gunsho Ruijū|Q1554852|WD=Q1554852|1=Gunsho Ruijū}} manuscript include "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin" among the 12 guardian shrines of Kōzuke, and this is identified with this shrine. On the other hand, the {{ill|lt=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)|Q17220813|WD=Q17220813|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)}} manuscript does not include it among the 10 guardian shrines. In the Kanra District section, the Ichinomiya manuscript has "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin," while the Gunsho Ruijū and Sōja manuscripts have "Junior First Rank, Sōgi Myōjin." {{ill|lt=Ozaki Kisao|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} regarded the guardian-shrine entry and district entry as different shrines, assigning the latter to Soki Shrine (Soki, Tomioka){{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=46,192-196}}. It was destroyed by fire during the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} era ({{ill|lt=1781|Q7719|WD=Q7719|1=1781}}-{{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772|1=1789}}), and the current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in April of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}){{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1801|Q6877|WD=Q6877|1=1801}})?{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The festival date was November 3, but is now April 3 and October 15. === Precincts === * Honden - one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. * Haiden * Mukuroji tree - 14 m tall, about 300 years old. Designated natural monument of Tomioka City. * Precinct shrines ** Akagi Shrine ** Takaokami Shrine ** Santai Shrine ** Shinmeigū ** Mishima Shrine ** Fuji Shrine ** Kotohira-gū ** Hokuto Shrine ** Ōikazuchi Shrine File:Uge-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Uge-jinja, shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Uge-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii File:Uge-jinja, mukuroji.jpg|Mukuroji tree (Tomioka City designated natural monument) ファイル:宇芸神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Cultural properties === === Tomioka City designated cultural properties === * Natural monument ** Mukuroji tree of Uge Shrine - designated April 28, 2010 (Heisei 22)[https://www.city.tomioka.lg.jp/www/contents/1000000000185/index.html Mukuroji Tree of Uge Shrine] (Tomioka City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Study of the Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |last=Ozaki |author=Ozaki Kisao |first=Kisao |author-link=尾崎喜左雄 |doi=10.11501/12266236 |ref=harv}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kita-Kanra District, Gunma Prefecture |date=1928-11-1 |year=1928 |publisher=Tamura Yasutarō |ref=harv |last=Honda |author=Honda Kamezō |first=Kamezō |doi=10.11501/3440451}}{{要登録}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Uge-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|Q11360308|WD=Q11360308|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uge Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:富岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tomioka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} era ({{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Uge Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uge Shrine''' (うげじんじゃ; {{ill|lt=kyūjitai|Q1147857|WD=Q1147857|1=Kyūjitai}}: 宇藝神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kannari, {{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835|1=Tomioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ){{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Tomioka City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Tomioka City |page=421 |doi=10.11501/9643274 |editor=Tomioka City History Editorial Committee |volume=Folklore volume}}{{要登録}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the Hakuhō era (late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The present shrine site was originally where Akagi Shrine stood, and Uge Shrine was originally on the west side near Yoshida Pond{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Uge Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kanra District|Q1076986|WD=Q1076986|1=Kanra District, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=40}}. In the ''Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō'', both the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Nukisaki Shrine|Q10863796|WD=Q10863796|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Nukisaki Shrine}} manuscript and the {{ill|lt=Gunsho Ruijū|Q1554852|WD=Q1554852|1=Gunsho Ruijū}} manuscript include "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin" among the 12 guardian shrines of Kōzuke, and this is identified with this shrine. On the other hand, the {{ill|lt=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)|Q17220813|WD=Q17220813|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)}} manuscript does not include it among the 10 guardian shrines. In the Kanra District section, the Ichinomiya manuscript has "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin," while the Gunsho Ruijū and Sōja manuscripts have "Junior First Rank, Sōgi Myōjin." {{ill|lt=Ozaki Kisao|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} regarded the guardian-shrine entry and district entry as different shrines, assigning the latter to Soki Shrine (Soki, Tomioka){{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=46,192-196}}. It was destroyed by fire during the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} era ({{ill|lt=1781|Q7719|WD=Q7719|1=1781}}-{{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772|1=1789}}), and the current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in April of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}){{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1801|Q6877|WD=Q6877|1=1801}})?{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The festival date was November 3, but is now April 3 and October 15. === Precincts === * Honden - one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. * Haiden * Mukuroji tree - 14 m tall, about 300 years old. Designated natural monument of Tomioka City. * Precinct shrines ** Akagi Shrine ** Takaokami Shrine ** Santai Shrine ** Shinmeigū ** Mishima Shrine ** Fuji Shrine ** Kotohira-gū ** Hokuto Shrine ** Ōikazuchi Shrine File:Uge-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Uge-jinja, shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Uge-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii File:Uge-jinja, mukuroji.jpg|Mukuroji tree (Tomioka City designated natural monument) ファイル:宇芸神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Cultural properties === === Tomioka City designated cultural properties === * Natural monument ** Mukuroji tree of Uge Shrine - designated April 28, 2010 (Heisei 22)[https://www.city.tomioka.lg.jp/www/contents/1000000000185/index.html Mukuroji Tree of Uge Shrine] (Tomioka City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Study of the Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |last=Ozaki |author=Ozaki Kisao |first=Kisao |author-link=尾崎喜左雄 |doi=10.11501/12266236 |ref=harv}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kita-Kanra District, Gunma Prefecture |date=1928-11-1 |year=1928 |publisher=Tamura Yasutarō |ref=harv |last=Honda |author=Honda Kamezō |first=Kamezō |doi=10.11501/3440451}}{{要登録}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Uge-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|Q11360308|WD=Q11360308|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uge Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:富岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tomioka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} era ({{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}) |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Uge Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uge Shrine''' (うげじんじゃ; {{ill|lt=kyūjitai|Q1147857|WD=Q1147857|1=Kyūjitai}}: 宇藝神社) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kannari, {{ill|lt=Tomioka|Q873835|WD=Q873835|1=Tomioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kura-inetama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} (うかのみたまのかみ){{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}{{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Tomioka City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Tomioka City |page=421 |doi=10.11501/9643274 |editor=Tomioka City History Editorial Committee |volume=Folklore volume}}{{要登録}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in the Hakuhō era (late {{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}}), in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The present shrine site was originally where Akagi Shrine stood, and Uge Shrine was originally on the west side near Yoshida Pond{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as "Uge Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kanra District|Q1076986|WD=Q1076986|1=Kanra District, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, and is ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=40}}. In the ''Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō'', both the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya Nukisaki Shrine|Q10863796|WD=Q10863796|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Nukisaki Shrine}} manuscript and the {{ill|lt=Gunsho Ruijū|Q1554852|WD=Q1554852|1=Gunsho Ruijū}} manuscript include "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin" among the 12 guardian shrines of Kōzuke, and this is identified with this shrine. On the other hand, the {{ill|lt=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)|Q17220813|WD=Q17220813|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja Shrine (Maebashi)}} manuscript does not include it among the 10 guardian shrines. In the Kanra District section, the Ichinomiya manuscript has "Junior First Rank, Ugi Daimyōjin," while the Gunsho Ruijū and Sōja manuscripts have "Junior First Rank, Sōgi Myōjin." {{ill|lt=Ozaki Kisao|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} regarded the guardian-shrine entry and district entry as different shrines, assigning the latter to Soki Shrine (Soki, Tomioka){{Sfn|Ozaki|1974|pp=46,192-196}}. It was destroyed by fire during the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} era ({{ill|lt=1781|Q7719|WD=Q7719|1=1781}}-{{ill|lt=1789|Q7772|WD=Q7772|1=1789}}), and the current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in April of {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1716|Q6892|WD=Q6892|1=1716}}){{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|WD=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1801|Q6877|WD=Q6877|1=1801}})?{{Sfn|Honda|1928|pp=817-819}}. The festival date was November 3, but is now April 3 and October 15. === Precincts === * Honden - one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. * Haiden * Mukuroji tree - 14 m tall, about 300 years old. Designated natural monument of Tomioka City. * Precinct shrines ** Akagi Shrine ** Takaokami Shrine ** Santai Shrine ** Shinmeigū ** Mishima Shrine ** Fuji Shrine ** Kotohira-gū ** Hokuto Shrine ** Ōikazuchi Shrine File:Uge-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden File:Uge-jinja, shaden.jpg|Haiden File:Uge-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Uge-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii File:Uge-jinja, mukuroji.jpg|Mukuroji tree (Tomioka City designated natural monument) ファイル:宇芸神社 一の鳥居.JPG|First torii === Cultural properties === === Tomioka City designated cultural properties === * Natural monument ** Mukuroji tree of Uge Shrine - designated April 28, 2010 (Heisei 22)[https://www.city.tomioka.lg.jp/www/contents/1000000000185/index.html Mukuroji Tree of Uge Shrine] (Tomioka City website).. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Study of the Kōzuke no Kuni Jinmyōchō |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |last=Ozaki |author=Ozaki Kisao |first=Kisao |author-link=尾崎喜左雄 |doi=10.11501/12266236 |ref=harv}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=History of Kita-Kanra District, Gunma Prefecture |date=1928-11-1 |year=1928 |publisher=Tamura Yasutarō |ref=harv |last=Honda |author=Honda Kamezō |first=Kamezō |doi=10.11501/3440451}}{{要登録}} === Related items === {{Commonscat|Uge-jinja}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|Q11360308|WD=Q11360308|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uge Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Uge Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:富岡市の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tomioka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]]
